surface_verification.justification: Framework-internal change: canonical .shipwright gitignore-block propagation (SSoT template + shared writer + adopt/project integration + drift test). No startable web/CLI/API product surface is affected. Empirical verification is the git check-ignore round-trips in a real git repo (shared/tests/test_gitignore_canon_merge.py: fresh-repo + webui-regression backfill prove runtime/ ignored & agent_docs/architecture.md tracked), the drift-congruence test (test_gitignore_template_congruent.py), and end-to-end runs of write-project-config.py + the adopt pipeline subprocess. Suites green: 22 new + adopt 296 + project 59.

degraded.detail: shared/tests/test_artifact_path_canon.py::test_no_legacy_artifact_paths[compliance-migrated] fails on the base commit (origin/main @ 55ac703), tripped by a PRIOR iterate's committed artifact .shipwright/agent_docs/iterates/iterate-2026-05-30-rtm-covered-ignore-untested-events.json line 8 ("adr": "compliance/rtm-status-logic" matches the legacy compliance/ canon regex). NOT introduced by this iterate (file not in this diff, tracked at base). Recommend a separate fix: add the value to ALLOWLIST['compliance'] or correct the slug.

shared/tests/test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact.py flags decision-drop iterate-2026-05-30-compliance-audit-on-stop_001.json (impact=convention) as missing an architecture.md entry. That drop belongs to a SEPARATE in-flight iterate and lives only in the main repo's gitignored decision-drops staging (not committed, not in this PR). THIS iterate's own drop (iterate-2026-05-30-gitignore-canon-propagation) IS reflected in architecture.md and passes. This failure vanishes on a clean CI checkout (the staging drop is gitignored). (The compliance-migrated canon-lint failure above, by contrast, is from a COMMITTED file so it persists on clean CI — but is likewise pre-existing and unrelated.) All other ~2575 shared tests pass; both failures are independent of this change.
